talk_metadata.talk_title: Origins of founder gene dosage mutations in normal breast epithelium and age‑related changes
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the vast majority of cells here, much less than 1% of the cells seem to have copy number gains"
Speaker said: "we don't know why we see fewer events in organoids – it's frustrating because we'd like to study this functionally"
Speaker said: "we think that this may represent lesions that were microscopically invisible as precursor lesions but were already, say, partially on their way to forming more established cancers"

key_data_gaps: No functional validation of the fitness advantage of 1q gains in normal cells
Limited longitudinal data linking aneuploid cells to eventual cancer development

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a CrisPRO‑based assay to quantify low‑frequency 1q gain and 10q loss in normal breast tissue as a risk predictor for future breast cancer.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we think that it may represent lesions that were microscopically invisible as precursor lesions but were already, say, partially on their way to forming more established cancers"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate single‑cell copy‑number data with chromatin accessibility to improve sensitivity for detecting early founder events.

crispro_opportunity.description: Use CrisPRO to distinguish age‑related aneuploidy from BRCA1/2‑associated changes for refined risk modeling in carriers.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we saw a slightly increased rate of copy number alterations ... in the heterozygotes"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Computational deconvolution of allele‑specific copy‑number could reveal subtle BRCA‑heterozygote signatures.

unresolved_questions: What is the precise timing of 1q gain relative to other driver mutations in normal epithelium?
Do aneuploid cells alter local immune surveillance before morphological changes appear?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Single‑cell DNA profiling of normal breast epithelium reveals age‑linked aneuploidy and lineage‑specific copy‑number events
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "about 5% of the epithelial cells were aneuploid"
Speaker said: "age was the only factor that we found that seemed to really correlate with increases of these cells"
Speaker said: "we didn't see a lot of copy‑neutral LOH"

key_data_gaps: Lack of longitudinal follow‑up to link aneuploid cells to cancer incidence
Limited functional assays to test oncogenic potential of specific CNAs

crispro_opportunity.description: Create a CrisPRO pipeline to integrate single‑cell DNA and ATAC data for early detection of lineage‑specific aneuploid clones in normal breast tissue.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we used a DNA‑and‑ATAC co‑assay method ... to look at which genes may be important within these chromosomal gains and losses"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can jointly model copy‑number and chromatin accessibility to prioritize driver genes within 1q, 10q, and 16q events.

crispro_opportunity.description: Design prospective cohort studies that stratify participants by aneuploid cell burden using CrisPRO‑derived risk scores.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "the big question ... is can these cells ... be used in some way to predict breast cancer risk?"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO risk scores could serve as enrollment criteria for prevention trials.

cited_competitors.name: Caris Therapeutics
cited_competitors.context: provided real‑world outcome data linking 9p loss and 1q gain to immunotherapy response

unresolved_questions: What molecular mechanisms drive the preferential occurrence of aneuploidy in luminal versus basal lineages?
Do aneuploid cells influence the stromal or immune microenvironment before morphological changes?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Aneuploidy patterns as predictors of immune evasion and therapy response across cancers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "9p loss is emerging as a strong predictor of immunoevasion across several tumor types"
Speaker said: "1q gain is associated with immune evasion and poor immunotherapy response in endometrial cancer"
Speaker said: "kidney cancer often behaves in the opposite way and has 1q gain associated with immune hot microenvironment"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Specific driver genes on 9p (e.g., antigen processing genes) not yet identified
Key immune‑modulatory genes on 1q (e.g., MDM4, NOTCH pathway) not fully resolved

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective validation of 9p loss and 1q gain as predictive biomarkers
Unclear causal genes within the large chromosomal arms

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop CrisPRO models to pinpoint the driver genes within 9p loss that mediate immune evasion, enabling a refined companion diagnostic.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we are building a computational prediction model ... to try to understand what are the main cytoband where immune evasion drivers are located"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate copy‑number, expression, and single‑cell ATAC to rank candidate genes within 9p.

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply CrisPRO to distinguish 1q‑gain associated innate immune suppression (macrophage depletion) across tumor types.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "1q gain ... is associated with a decreased abundance of innate immune cells, specifically macrophages"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Modeling of copy‑number effects on macrophage‑related pathways can generate a predictive signature for ICI response.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate CrisPRO‑derived 9p/1q status into stratification arms of upcoming immunotherapy trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "9p loss was predictive of poorer survival in patients treated with immune checkpoint inhibitors"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Provides a quantitative, reproducible metric for patient selection.

cited_competitors.name: Caris Therapeutics
cited_competitors.context: provided real‑world data showing 9p loss predicts poor ICI response

unresolved_questions: Which specific genes on 9p and 1q drive the observed immune evasion phenotypes?
Why does 1q gain correlate with immune cold microenvironment in most cancers but immune hot in kidney cancer?
